title: Advanced: Deep Learning and Deployment
description: Understand neural networks, model deployment patterns, and responsible monitoring.

sections.title: Neural Network Building Blocks
sections.intro: Neural networks stack layers of weighted computations and non-linear activations to learn complex patterns.
sections.example: An image classifier may use convolutional layers to detect edges, textures, then object-level structures.
sections.exercise: Describe in simple words what an activation function adds compared to linear-only layers.

sections.title: Optimization and Regularization
sections.intro: Gradient-based optimization updates weights. Regularization methods (dropout, weight decay) improve generalization.
sections.example: Dropout randomly disables neurons during training to reduce dependence on specific feature paths.
sections.exercise: Name one sign in training logs that suggests your model is overfitting.

sections.title: Deployment and Monitoring
sections.intro: Production ML requires versioning, latency checks, drift monitoring, and feedback loops.
sections.example: A fraud model can degrade if customer behavior changes; drift alerts trigger model retraining.
sections.exercise: List two metrics you would monitor after deploying a model API.

references: Adapted from open deep-learning concepts in public educational material (including Wikipedia entries).
Adapted from publicly available MLOps deployment and monitoring primers.
